mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses statistical significance after controlling for inflation trends.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust household purchasing power without monetary policy tightening pressure, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually, or the interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and rising corporate revenue streams, which immediately bid up equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during periods of simultaneous monetary tightening, or out-of-sample hit rate drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher aggregate demand, which consistently translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 24-month Rank IC falls below 0.01, or positive payroll surprises yield negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.0 million units annually indicates a collapsing construction sector and weakening consumer confidence, which drags down equity markets over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters when HOUST drops below 1000.0, or the threshold effect vanishes after adjusting for regional interest rate differentials.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate occurring alongside accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns when inflation exceeds 2.5 percent, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0 percent coincides with negative equity returns in more than 50% of quarters, or the signal becomes statistically insignificant after controlling for lagged market momentum.
